Title: Diacylglyceryl-N,N,N-trimethylhomoserine-dependent lipid remodeling in a green alga, Chlorella kessleri

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Chlorella kessleri homolog identified using Chlamydomonas-related annotation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports phospholipid degradation to release diacylglycerol and phosphorus for DGTS-based lipid remodeling under P deficiency
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glycerophosphodiester phosphodiesterase-like protein; candidate PLC-like enzyme
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strictly repressed under +P and upregulated over time after shift to -P
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced by phosphorus starvation in parallel with phospholipid degradation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely promotes degradation of PC and PE and thereby supports DGTS accumulation and phosphate remobilization
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Genome homology search; semiquantitative RT-PCR correlation with lipid remodeling
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome homology search; no accession reported for C. kessleri locus
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PLCC1 is one of two GDPD-like/possible PLC candidate genes in C. kessleri. 2. Unlike PLCC2, PLCC1 is strongly induced by phosphorus starvation. 3. Its induction matches the timing of PC and PE degradation, implicating it in release of DG and phosphate for DGTS remodeling.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Direct biochemical activity was not demonstrated; may be GDPD rather than true PLC
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Authors explicitly state the protein may not be a true PLC and actual enzymatic activity awaits future study

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Cellular phosphate pool / DGTS remodeling-associated phosphorus recycling
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Likely releases inorganic phosphate from phosphocholine and phosphoethanolamine generated during phospholipid breakdown under P starvation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Almost undetectable under +P and markedly upregulated under -P
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Likely supports Pi scavenging/recycling from phospholipid degradation products during lipid remodeling
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Semiquantitative RT-PCR correlation with lipid remodeling model
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PLP transcript is strongly induced by phosphorus starvation from an almost undetectable basal level. 2. Its induction is consistent with a role in dephosphorylating phospholipid headgroup breakdown products. 3. PLP likely contributes to Pi release that supports acclimation to P deficiency.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Function inferred from expression and pathway logic, not directly validated biochemically
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Proposed to act downstream of PLCC1-mediated phospholipid cleavage to release Pi

Common_Genes.Source_Species: Chlorella kessleri homolog identified using Chlamydomonas-related annotation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Candidate phospholipid degradation gene examined in the study but not responsive to P starvation
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glycerophosphodiester phosphodiesterase-like protein; candidate PLC-like enzyme
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Little affected irrespective of phosphorus conditions
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: No clear response to phosphorus starvation
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Not supported as a major contributor to phosphorus starvation-induced DGTS remodeling
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Genome homology search; no accession reported for C. kessleri locus
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. PLCC2 is a second GDPD-like/possible PLC candidate in C. kessleri. 2. Its transcript level showed little change under phosphorus starvation. 3. Therefore, it is unlikely to be the major phospholipid-degradation factor in the observed DGTS remodeling response.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because experimentally evaluated in Results, but evidence argues against a core causal role compared with PLCC1

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. ACT served only as an internal RT-PCR control.
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Not a nutrient metabolism core gene; included only as assay control if strict completeness is desired

Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Non-phosphorus zwitterionic membrane lipid that substitutes for extrachloroplast phospholipids during phosphorus deficiency and helps lower cellular P quota
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Extraplastid zwitterionic membrane lipid replacement branch under phosphorus starvation
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: BTA1 fusion protein with N-terminal BtaB-like domain and C-terminal BtaA-like domain; type B BTA1; conserved SAM-binding VD motifs
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Diacylglycerol; S-adenosylmethionine; homoserine-derived donor for DGTS headgroup formation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes DGTS formation on a diacylglycerol backbone through sequential amino-carboxypropyl transfer and methylation reactions mediated by fused BtaA-like and BtaB-like domains
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control point under phosphorus deficiency
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Very low under +P; strongly induced over time after shift to -P
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Very low under phosphorus replete conditions and markedly upregulated during phosphorus starvation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes strong DGTS accumulation under phosphorus starvation, enabling substitution of PC and PE and contributing to phosphorus-deficiency acclimation
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Phosphorus starvation (-P); DGTS accumulation also depends on photosynthesis and is strongly repressed by DCMU, darkness, and cycloheximide
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: 5'/3' RACE cloning; sequence/domain analysis; heterologous expression in E. coli followed by TLC and LC/MS2 identification of DGTS; semiquantitative RT-PCR; lipid profiling
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Genomic DNA database search of C. kessleri; no accession provided for genome; nucleotide sequence deposited in GenBank: LC648247
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: IPTG-inducible expression in E. coli using pMAL-c5X for functional validation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Requires nitrogen-containing DGTS headgroup and photosynthetic activity; may alter membrane composition and interact with PC/PE turnover and triacylglycerol accumulation
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. CkBTA1 encodes the DGTS synthase responsible for de novo DGTS production in C. kessleri. 2. Its transcript is nearly absent in +P cells but strongly induced by phosphorus starvation in parallel with DGTS accumulation. 3. Heterologous expression in E. coli generated DGTS, proving enzyme function. 4. CkBTA1-driven DGTS synthesis supports replacement of PC and PE during lipid remodeling under -P.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: DGTS synthesized in C. kessleri is mainly eukaryotic type based on sn-2 C18 acyl composition, implying synthesis via ER-associated eukaryotic pathway; gene inferred to be nuclear encoded
